name: Main
description: Session-scoped preflight rules via SIGNET_SESSION. Adds session_id to the Preflight struct and threads session-awareness through all preflight operations (submit, query, deactivation, history, hook evaluation) so that concurrent Claude Code instances in different projects do not interfere with each other's preflights. When SIGNET_SESSION is unset, all behavior is backward-compatible with the current global-scope model. DB schema migration adds a nullable session_id TEXT column and index, wrapped in a transaction with hook-safe error handling.

types.name: SessionId
types.description: Opaque string identifier for an evaluation session.

types.name: PreflightSessionFilter
types.description: Determines how preflight queries are scoped. Computed from SIGNET_SESSION at each call site via current_session_filter(). Scoped(sid): match preflights with session_id = sid OR session_id IS NULL. Unscoped: match any active preflight (backward-compatible behavior when SIGNET_SESSION is unset).

types.name: PreflightDeactivationScope
types.description: Controls which preflights are deactivated when a new one is filed. ExactSession(sid): deactivate only WHERE session_id = sid. GlobalOnly: deactivate only WHERE session_id IS NULL. Derived from the incoming preflight's session_id field.

types.name: Preflight

validators.error_message: Preflight id must be a valid UUID v4

fields.name: task_description
fields.description: Human-readable description of the task this preflight constrains.

fields.name: filed_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the preflight was filed.

fields.name: active
fields.description: Whether this preflight is currently active. Only one preflight per (session_id) scope may be active at a time.

fields.name: session_id
fields.description: Session scope for this preflight. None means global scope (applies to all sessions). When set, this preflight only applies to the matching session. Serialized with #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] to preserve HMAC compatibility with pre-migration preflights.

fields.name: hmac
fields.description: HMAC-SHA256 covering the serialized preflight payload (including session_id when present). Ensures tamper detection.

types.description: A self-imposed constraint record filed before starting work. Stored in SQLite preflights table. HMAC covers the full serialized payload including session_id when present.

types.name: PreflightList
types.description: A list of Preflight records, used for history queries.

types.name: PreflightHistoryFilter

fields.name: session_filter
fields.description: Session scope filter. When Scoped, returns preflights matching that session_id plus global preflights. When Unscoped, returns all preflights.

fields.name: limit
fields.description: Maximum number of preflights to return, ordered by filed_at DESC.

fields.name: active_only
fields.description: If true, return only active preflights.

types.description: Optional filtering parameters for preflight history queries.

types.name: McpToolResponse

fields.name: session_id
fields.description: The session_id that was captured or matched. Echoed in responses so callers can verify session scope.

types.description: Standard MCP tool response structure for preflight operations.

types.name: MigrationResult
types.description: Outcome of the database schema migration for session_id support.

types.name: PreflightError
types.description: Error types produced by preflight operations.

functions.name: current_session_id
functions.description: Reads the SIGNET_SESSION environment variable and returns an OptionalSessionId. Returns None if the env var is unset or empty. Single canonical implementation; called at each use site, never cached. Equivalent to: std::env::var("SIGNET_SESSION").ok().filter(|s| !s.is_empty()).
postconditions: Returns None if SIGNET_SESSION is unset
Returns None if SIGNET_SESSION is set to empty string
Returns Some(sid) where sid is non-empty if SIGNET_SESSION is set to a non-empty value
Result is not cached — each call re-reads the environment

structured_side_effects.description: Reads process environment; no filesystem or network I/O

functions.name: current_session_filter
functions.description: Derives a PreflightSessionFilter from the current SIGNET_SESSION env var. Returns Scoped(sid) when SIGNET_SESSION is set and non-empty, Unscoped otherwise. Convenience wrapper around current_session_id().
postconditions: Returns Scoped(sid) iff current_session_id() returns Some(sid)
Returns Unscoped iff current_session_id() returns None

functions.name: migrate_preflight_schema
functions.description: Ensures the preflights table has the session_id TEXT column and the idx_preflight_session index. Checks PRAGMA table_info(preflights) for column existence. If absent, executes ALTER TABLE preflights ADD COLUMN session_id TEXT within a transaction. Always executes CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_preflight_session ON preflights(session_id). In hook mode, errors are caught and logged (never propagated) to maintain exit code 0 invariant.

inputs.name: db_connection
inputs.description: Open rusqlite::Connection to the preflight SQLite database.

inputs.name: hook_mode
inputs.description: When true, migration errors are logged and swallowed rather than propagated.

error_cases.name: migration_sql_error
error_cases.condition: ALTER TABLE or CREATE INDEX SQL statement fails

error_data.detail: SQLite error message from failed DDL statement

preconditions: db_connection is a valid, open SQLite connection
preflights table already exists (base schema created by vault init)
postconditions: On MigrationApplied: session_id TEXT column exists on preflights table
On MigrationApplied: idx_preflight_session index exists on preflights(session_id)
On AlreadyMigrated: no schema changes made
On MigrationFailed with hook_mode=true: error is logged, function returns MigrationFailed without panicking
Existing rows have session_id = NULL after migration
No data in existing rows is modified

structured_side_effects.description: Adds session_id TEXT column and index if not already present
Logs migration status and any errors encountered in hook mode

functions.name: store_preflight
functions.description: Persists a new Preflight to the SQLite database. Before inserting, deactivates previous preflights with exact session_id match: if the incoming preflight has session_id = Some(sid), deactivates only WHERE session_id = sid AND active = 1; if session_id = None, deactivates only WHERE session_id IS NULL AND active = 1. This ensures session-scoped filings never deactivate global preflights and vice versa. The deactivation and insert are wrapped in a single transaction.

inputs.name: db_connection
inputs.description: Open rusqlite::Connection to the preflight SQLite database.

inputs.name: preflight
inputs.description: The preflight record to store. Must have HMAC already computed (covering session_id).

error_cases.name: database_write_error
error_cases.condition: SQLite INSERT or UPDATE fails (disk full, locked, etc.)

preconditions: preflight.hmac is a valid HMAC-SHA256 over the serialized payload including session_id
preflight.session_id is either None or a non-empty string
migrate_preflight_schema has been called on this connection
postconditions: The new preflight is persisted with active = 1
All previously active preflights with the SAME session_id are now active = 0
Preflights with a DIFFERENT session_id are unmodified
Global preflights (session_id IS NULL) are unmodified when incoming preflight has session_id = Some(_)
Session-scoped preflights are unmodified when incoming preflight has session_id = None
The entire operation (deactivate + insert) is atomic (single transaction)

structured_side_effects.description: Deactivates same-scope preflights and inserts new active preflight

functions.name: active_preflight
functions.description: Queries the SQLite database for the currently active preflight, scoped by session. When filter is Scoped(sid): SELECT ... WHERE active = 1 AND (session_id = ?1 OR session_id IS NULL) ORDER BY filed_at DESC LIMIT 1. When filter is Unscoped: SELECT ... WHERE active = 1 ORDER BY filed_at DESC LIMIT 1. Returns the most recently filed matching active preflight, or None if no active preflight exists in scope.

inputs.name: db_connection
inputs.description: Open rusqlite::Connection to the preflight SQLite database.

inputs.name: filter
inputs.description: Session scope filter. Scoped(sid) matches session-specific and global preflights; Unscoped matches any active preflight.

error_cases.name: deserialization_error
error_cases.condition: Stored preflight row cannot be deserialized into Preflight struct

error_data.detail: Failed to deserialize preflight from database row

preconditions: migrate_preflight_schema has been called on this connection
postconditions: If Some(p) returned: p.active == true
If Some(p) returned and filter is Scoped(sid): p.session_id == Some(sid) OR p.session_id == None
If None returned: no active preflight exists matching the filter criteria
When filter is Unscoped: behavior is identical to pre-session-scoping implementation
HMAC of returned preflight is not verified by this function (caller responsibility)

structured_side_effects.description: Reads active preflight rows filtered by session scope

functions.name: is_preflight_locked
functions.description: Returns whether there is an active preflight in the current session scope. Delegates to active_preflight(current_session_filter()). Returns true if an active preflight exists, false otherwise. Used by hook evaluation to determine if preflight constraints apply.

inputs.name: db_connection
inputs.description: Open rusqlite::Connection to the preflight SQLite database.

preconditions: migrate_preflight_schema has been called on this connection
postconditions: Returns true iff active_preflight(db_connection, current_session_filter()) returns Some(_)
Returns false iff active_preflight(db_connection, current_session_filter()) returns None

structured_side_effects.target: SQLite preflights table and SIGNET_SESSION env var
structured_side_effects.description: Reads environment variable and queries database

functions.name: preflight_history
functions.description: Returns a list of preflights ordered by filed_at DESC, optionally filtered by session scope and active status. When filter.session_filter is Scoped(sid): returns preflights where session_id = sid OR session_id IS NULL. When Unscoped: returns all preflights.

inputs.name: db_connection
inputs.description: Open rusqlite::Connection to the preflight SQLite database.

inputs.name: filter
inputs.description: Filtering and pagination parameters for the history query.

preconditions: migrate_preflight_schema has been called on this connection
postconditions: Returned list length <= filter.limit
Returned list is ordered by filed_at DESC
If filter.active_only is true: all returned preflights have active == true
If filter.session_filter is Scoped(sid): all returned preflights have session_id == Some(sid) OR session_id == None

structured_side_effects.description: Reads preflight history rows with optional session and active filters

functions.name: handle_preflight_submit
functions.description: MCP tool handler for signet_preflight_submit. Reads SIGNET_SESSION via current_session_id(), sets it on the Preflight struct, computes HMAC over the full serialized payload (including session_id), then delegates to store_preflight(). Returns an McpToolResponse echoing the captured session_id.

inputs.name: db_connection
inputs.description: Open rusqlite::Connection to the preflight SQLite database.

inputs.name: task_description
inputs.description: Human-readable task description for the preflight.

validators.error_message: At least one constraint must be provided

error_data.detail: At least one constraint must be provided

preconditions: migrate_preflight_schema has been called on this connection
postconditions: On success: response.success == true
On success: response.preflight is the stored Preflight with HMAC computed
On success: response.session_id matches current_session_id() at time of call
On success: response.preflight.session_id == response.session_id
Previous active preflights with the same session_id are deactivated
Previous active preflights with a different session_id are unmodified

structured_side_effects.description: Deactivates same-scope preflights and inserts new active preflight

functions.name: handle_preflight_active
functions.description: MCP tool handler for signet_preflight_active. Reads current session via current_session_filter(), delegates to active_preflight(), returns McpToolResponse with the active preflight (if any) including its session_id field.

inputs.name: db_connection
inputs.description: Open rusqlite::Connection to the preflight SQLite database.

preconditions: migrate_preflight_schema has been called on this connection
postconditions: response.success == true (even if no active preflight exists)
If active preflight exists in scope: response.preflight is set and response.session_id reflects its session_id
If no active preflight in scope: response.message indicates none active, response.preflight is not set

structured_side_effects.target: environment variable SIGNET_SESSION and SQLite preflights table
structured_side_effects.description: Reads session scope from environment, queries active preflight from database

functions.name: evaluate_preflight_in_hook
functions.description: Hook-mode preflight evaluation. Reads current session scope, queries active_preflight, and evaluates whether the pending tool call is consistent with the active preflight's constraints. In hook mode, ALL errors are caught and logged — the function NEVER propagates errors and NEVER causes a non-zero exit code. If no active preflight matches the session scope, evaluation is a no-op pass.

inputs.name: db_connection
inputs.description: Open rusqlite::Connection to the preflight SQLite database.

inputs.name: tool_name
inputs.description: Name of the tool being invoked in the hook.

inputs.name: tool_input
inputs.description: Serialized JSON input to the tool being invoked.

postconditions: Function NEVER panics
Function NEVER returns an error that would cause non-zero exit code
If SIGNET_SESSION is set: only preflights matching that session (or global) are considered
If SIGNET_SESSION is unset: any active preflight is considered (backward compatible)
If no active preflight in scope: response.success == true with pass-through message
If database errors occur: they are logged to stderr and the tool call is allowed (fail-open in hook mode)

structured_side_effects.description: Queries active preflight for the current session scope
Logs any errors encountered during evaluation (errors are swallowed, not propagated)

invariants: When SIGNET_SESSION is unset or empty, all preflight behavior is identical to pre-session-scoping implementation (backward compatible)
session_id stored in the database is always either NULL or a non-empty string — empty strings are normalized to NULL
Only one preflight per unique session_id value may be active at any time (enforced by store_preflight deactivation)
Global preflights (session_id IS NULL) are visible in all session scopes (Scoped queries match session_id = sid OR session_id IS NULL)

Session-scoped deactivation uses exact match semantics: filing with session_id = X deactivates only active preflights with session_id = X; filing with session_id = NULL deactivates only active preflights with session_id IS NULL
HMAC covers the full serialized Preflight payload including session_id when present
skip_serializing_if = Option::is_none on session_id ensures pre-migration preflights with session_id = None serialize identically to their original form, preserving HMAC validity
In hook mode, exit code is always 0 regardless of database errors, migration failures, or query failures
migrate_preflight_schema is idempotent and safe to call on every connection open
No unsafe code is used anywhere in the implementation
All 138 existing tests continue to pass without modification
Policy evaluation (policy.rs) is not modified — it remains deterministic and side-effect-free
No new crate dependencies are introduced

data_access.rationale: Reads and writes preflight constraint records (task descriptions, self-imposed constraints, session identifiers, HMAC signatures) in a local SQLite database. Reads the SIGNET_SESSION environment variable (derived from $PWD) to determine session scope. All data is internal operational state — no PII, no credentials, no external user data. The preflight data includes task descriptions authored by the AI agent and constraint strings, both classified as INTERNAL operational metadata.

side_effects.rationale: Reads SIGNET_SESSION environment variable to determine session scope for preflight filtering and capture. Value is typically a filesystem path ($PWD) set at Claude Code launch.
Queries preflight records from SQLite to check active constraints, retrieve history, and evaluate hook compliance. Filtered by session_id for scope isolation.
Inserts new preflight records and deactivates prior same-scope preflights. Schema migration adds session_id column and index.
ALTER TABLE to add session_id TEXT column and CREATE INDEX for efficient session-scoped queries. Migration is idempotent.

authority.rationale: This component is the sole authority for session-scoped preflight management. It owns the semantics of how SIGNET_SESSION maps to preflight visibility, the deactivation rules (exact session_id match), the schema migration for session_id support, and the invariant that global preflights (NULL session_id) are visible across all sessions. No other component defines or interprets preflight session scoping.
